Here's what I found about the **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** from the loisirs74.fr page and other sources:

## Overview
The Plage de Saint-Jorioz is a large sandy beach on the shore of Lake Annecy facing the Dents de Lanfon. It's the only beach around Lake Annecy to offer a large sandy beach. It holds the "Blue Flag" label since 2010 and is also labeled Tourism & Handicap for accessibility.

## Opening Hours & Access
According to loisirs74.fr:
- January 1 – June 30: free access every day.
- July 1 – August 31: paid access from 9:30 am to 5:30 pm (supervised swimming by lifeguards). Closed 7 am–9:30 am for cleaning.
- September 1 – December 31: free access every day.

## Pricing (July–August only)
Adult (18+): €2.60. Child 10–17 years: €1. Under 10: free. Entry after 4:30 pm: €1 (all ages). Season pass (unlimited access July–Aug): €22. There is also a smart card support (creation fee): €2. Access is free from September to June.

## Tickets
The beach does not require reservation. You buy your entry ticket on-site at the cash desks (July–August), or online via the ticketing system on saint-jorioz.fr (smart card, package, or single ticket). Outside July–August, access is free.

## Facilities & Activities
The beach is well-equipped with a diving board, shaded lawns, children's play area/paddling pool, beach volleyball court, and pétanque. It also has a restaurant and snack bar, plus a natural sandy area, showers, and free parking. Nearby you can rent pedalos, paddles, catamarans, and enjoy water-skiing, wakeboarding, and surfing.

For visitors with reduced mobility: the beach is equipped with an access ramp into the water and two amphibious wheelchairs (tiralo + hippocampe chairs) provided free of charge during July and August.

## Getting There
From Annecy (9 km, about 15 min off-season), follow the west shore of Lake Annecy via the D909a toward Duingt/Faverges. In Saint-Jorioz, turn left at the light toward the beach (signposted). Large free parking in front of the beach entrance. Note: there are frequent traffic jams in summer at the end of the day returning to Annecy. Alternatives include the SIBRA bus line 15 from Annecy, seasonal nautical shuttles and cruise boats from Annecy, and the Lake Annecy cycle path.

## Good to Know
- Entry is prohibited for children under 10 unaccompanied by an adult. Dogs are prohibited within the beach enclosure all year, even on a leash.
- However, dogs are accepted on the small beach to the left of the Plage de Saint-Jorioz — one of the few beaches at Annecy where you can bring your dog (during the season).

**Note:** The information on loisirs74.fr was verified on the official site on May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.prompt: Extract the entry/admission price (tarif), the opening hours (horaires), and the wheelchair accessibility (accessibilité PMR / fauteuil roulant) for this beach. Report exactly what the page states for each.

content.content:
# Plage de Saint-Jorioz: Key Information

**Admission Price (Tarif):**
"Adulte (18 ans et +) : 2,60 € · Enfant 10–17 ans : 1 € · Moins de 10 ans : gratuit"
Outside summer months (September-June), access is complimentary.

**Opening Hours (Horaires):**
The beach operates year-round with seasonal variations. It's freely accessible January 30-June and September 31-December. From July 1-August 31, paid entry is available from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM, with a 7:00-9:30 AM closure for cleaning.

**Wheelchair Accessibility (Accessibilité PMR):**
"Rampe d'accès à l'eau, tapis avec main courante et deux fauteuils amphibies (tiralo + hippocampe) gratuits en juillet-août ; entrée de plain-pied, WC adaptés, place PMR."
The facility holds the "Tourisme et Handicap" certification, indicating comprehensive disability accommodations including water access ramps and complimentary amphibious wheelchairs during peak season.

content.text:
I researched the loisirs74.fr page for Plage de Saint-Jorioz. Here are the three requested facts:

- **Entry price:** Adults (18+) €2.60 · Children 10–17 €1 · Under 10 free (paid entry applies in July–August only; free access September–June).
- **Opening hours:** Open year-round. Free access January–June and September–December. In July–August, paid entry from **9:30 AM to 5:30 PM**, with a closure from 7:00–9:30 AM for cleaning.
- **Wheelchair accessibility:** Accessible — water-access ramp, matting with handrail, and two free amphibious wheelchairs (tiralo + hippocampe) in July–August; step-free entrance, adapted WC, and PMR parking space. Holds the "Tourisme et Handicap" label.

Source: [Plage de Saint-Jorioz · Tarifs et horaires — loisirs74.fr](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz)

Note: I only browsed and read the page as instructed — no forms or bookings were submitted. Details are seasonal and subject to change.
